BiomasBR · PRODES-AMZ Project

SITS-AMZ Pipeline

End-to-end deforestation and degradation mapping for the Brazilian Legal Amazon

15 Scripts SITS ≥ 1.6.0

This guide documents the complete processing pipeline of the PRODES-AMZ project, an application of the sits R package for annual deforestation and forest degradation monitoring in the Brazilian Legal Amazon. The pipeline processes Sentinel-2 16-day composites from the Brazil Data Cube (BDC), covering neighboring tiles: 012014, 012015, 013014, and 013015 for the 2024-08 to 2025-07 reference period.

The pipeline follows a time-first, space-later paradigm: each spatial unit is associated with a multivariate time series of spectral bands, a machine learning model classifies those time series, and spatial post-processing rules produce PRODES-compatible polygon outputs. Classification is performed at segment level (Object-Based Image Analysis) using vector data cubes.

📖 Note

All scripts are available at github.com/migualex/sits-prodes. For theoretical background, refer to the SITS Book (Simoes et al., 2021).

0.1Pipeline Overview

#ScriptDescription
011_mixture_model.RGenerate Linear Spectral Mixture Model fraction bands
022_1_segmentation.RSNIC-based segmentation
033_1_time_series_sampling.RExtract training time series
043_2_samples_quality_assessment.RSOM quality assessment
054_1_model_training_random_forest.RRandom Forest training
064_2_model_training_xgboost.RXGBoost training
074_3_model_training_tempcnn.RTempCNN training
084_4_model_training_ltae.RLTAE training
095_classification.RClassify vector data cube
106_1_validation_samples_stratification.RStratified sample design for Forest area validation
116_2_validation_samples_stratification_nf.RStratified sample design for Non-Forest area validation
127_1_validation_accuracy_measurement.RValidation accuracy measurement for Forest area
137_2_validation_accuracy_measurement_nf.RValidation accuracy measurement for Non-Forest area
148_1_post_processing.RPost-processing for Forest area
158_2_post_processing_nf.RPost-processing for Non-Forest area
Next →Introduction
Chapter 1

Introduction

Context, paradigm, and key concepts

1.1PRODES and BiomasBR

The PRODES program, operated by INPE, has continuously monitored deforestation in the Brazilian Legal Amazon since 1988. PRODES tracks gross annual deforestation — defined as primary forest removed within each August-to-July reference year — using Landsat-class imagery and polygon-based shapefiles with a 1 ha minimum mapping unit. This pipeline extends PRODES-compatible mapping to include forest degradation classes (Degradacao, Degradacao_Por_Fogo) alongside the standard deforestation classes, using Sentinel-2 time series and object-based classification.

1.2Time-First, Space-Later

The sits package implements the time-first, space-later paradigm (SITS Book, Ch. 1). In the time-first phase, each pixel or segment is represented as a multivariate time series across all acquisition dates; a machine learning model then classifies these time series. In the space-later phase, spatial post-processing rules refine the map. This approach captures seasonal phenological signals — such as the NDVI cycle of pasture versus native vegetation — that single-date composites cannot discriminate.

1.3Object-Based Classification with Vector Data Cubes

Rather than classifying individual pixels, this pipeline segments the image into spatially homogeneous objects (SNIC superpixels), assembles them into a vector data cube (SITS Book, Ch. 25), and classifies the time series extracted per segment. This eliminates salt-and-pepper noise, produces polygon outputs compatible with the PRODES data model, and allows enforcing the minimum mapping unit before classification by controlling segment size.

1.4Brazil Data Cube as Data Source

All imagery is sourced from the Brazil Data Cube (BDC) collection SENTINEL-2-16D, which provides analysis-ready 16-day best-pixel composites at 10 m resolution. The BDC applies cloud masking and compositing, making the cubes directly suitable for time series analysis without additional preprocessing. Bands used: B02, B03, B04, B05, B06, B07, B08, B8A, B11, B12, NDVI, NBR, EVI, CLOUD.

Chapter 2

Setup

Installation, dependencies, and project structure

2.1Installation

R 
# Install sits from CRAN (stable release)
install.packages("sits")  # requires sits >= 1.5.1

# Or install the development version from GitHub
# devtools::install_github("e-sensing/sits", dependencies = TRUE)

# Install all supporting packages used in this pipeline
install.packages(c(
  "sf", "terra", "dplyr", "tibble", "ggplot2", "lubridate",
  "purrr", "stringr", "fs", "RColorBrewer", "gdalUtilities",
  "pals", "cli", "units", "smoothr", "torch", "luz"
))
⚠ BDC Access Token

All cubes are sourced from the Brazil Data Cube (source = "BDC"). A free access token is required. Register at BDC and configure with sits_config(bdc_access_key = "...").

2.2Libraries

Every R package used across all scripts, with a brief description of its role in the pipeline.

PackageDescriptionUsed in
sitsProvides all SITS functions: data cube creation, Linear Spectral Mixture Model, segmentation, SOM quality control, ML training, classification, uncertainty estimation, and accuracy assessment.All scripts
sfStandard R interface for vector spatial data. Used for reading/writing GeoPackage files, geometric operations (union, difference, buffer, area calculation), and CRS management.2.1, 6.1, 6.2, 7.2, 8.2
terraRaster data processing. Used to rasterize classified segment vectors, compute entropy rasters from vector uncertainty outputs, and write compressed GeoTIFF files.5, 6.1, 6.2, 7.2, 8.2
dplyrData manipulation. Provides filter, mutate, group_by, summarise, bind_rows, and pipe-based workflows for sample tables and spatial data frames.3.1, 3.2, 6.1, 6.2, 7.2, 8.2
tibbleModern data frame format required by sits for training sample objects. Also used to construct the endmember specification table in Script 1.1, 3.1, 3.2, 6.1, 6.2
ggplot2Plotting. Generates SOM evaluation charts, confusion matrices, per-class accuracy heatmaps, error-adjusted area bar charts, and OOB error curves.3.1, 3.2, 4.1, 4.2, 4.4, 7.2
lubridateDate arithmetic. Computes the number of years between start_date and end_date used to generate version strings in output filenames.2.1, 3.1, 3.2, 4.1, 4.2, 5
purrrFunctional iteration over lists and vectors. Used to map rasterization and reclassification functions across multiple tiles and cube directories.6.1, 6.2, 7.2, 8.2
stringrString manipulation. Extracts tile IDs and period codes from version strings to automatically locate the correct cube directories.6.1, 6.2, 7.2
fsFile system operations. Creates directories, expands paths, and manages temporary files during rasterization workflows.6.1, 6.2
RColorBrewerColor palettes for uncertainty maps and for the class style tables written as QGIS-compatible QML files alongside rasterized outputs.5, 6.1, 6.2
gdalUtilitiesWraps GDAL's gdal_rasterize to convert classified segment vector maps to integer-coded GeoTIFF rasters that sits_cube() can read for accuracy assessment.6.1, 6.2
palsExtended color palettes. Provides cols25() for assigning visually distinct colors to 13+ land cover classes in the rasterized output style file.6.1, 6.2
cliCommand-line interface messages. Reports per-tile progress during rasterization and reclassification loops with formatted, colored status output.6.1, 6.2, 7.2
unitsPhysical unit handling for spatial thresholds. Used to specify area filters in square metres as typed quantities for smoothr::fill_holes().8.2
smoothrPolygon smoothing and hole filling. fill_holes() removes internal holes below a given area threshold from classified suppression polygons after cloud removal.8.2
torchDeep learning backend for R. Required by sits_lighttae() for LTAE model training. Provides GPU acceleration via CUDA when available.4.4
luzHigh-level training loop for torch. Manages epochs, callbacks, and learning rate schedulers used internally by the LTAE training routine.4.4

2.3Project Structure

bash 
prodes.amz/
├── scripts/                    
├── data/
│   ├── raw/
│   │   ├── mixture_model/      
│   │   ├── samples/            
│   │   │   └── validation_samples/  
│   │   └── auxiliary/          
│   ├── segments/               
│   ├── rds/
│   │   ├── time_series/        
│   │   ├── som/                
│   │   └── model/
│   │       ├── random_forest/  
│   │       ├── xgboost/        
│   │       └── tempcnn/           
│   │       └── ltae/           
│   ├── class/                  
│   │   ├── raster/             
│   │   └── post_process/       
│   └── plots/
